content: Just as linguistic reflection (the meaning of the word from the high school English vocabulary) acts as a meta-layer that mirrors how language encodes ideas about thinking itself, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by grounding their self-critique loops in structured vocabulary practice of reflective terminology (e.g., "error", "reconsider", "alternate approach") drawn from second-language learning curricula. This builds a more consistent internal framing for self-assessment that mirrors how humans learn to explicitly practice reflective thinking, rather than leaving it as an emergent unstructured ability.
